markdownRemark.html:
Hello, friend!

Hello there friend, I'm Matt - and welcome to my blog.

Disclaimer

Cards firmly on the table, I've never been that great at keeping a blog up to date. Previous data would suggest that my posting is sporadic at best, and matching the cadence of a regional eclipse at worse. I'm hoping this is an opportunity to draw a line in the sand and make updates a more regular occurance.

You might be asking, and rightedly so, the same question I asked myself "Matt, c'mon, why will this be any different?"

Well, my life has markedly changed since my last attempt at keeping a regular blog - honest!

Why am I writing a blog?

Since 2017 my life got flipped-turned upside down, and I'd like to take a minute, sit right there, I'll tell you how I blogging became a breath of fresh air.

cough excuse me cough

Blogging for me is a way to share interesting discoveries, creations, and tid bits with a wider audience. Often as a student, developer, or engineer, we find ourselves, similar to artists, rummaging through supplies, studying new techniques, looking at new ways of approaching our canvas. Instead of consuming this information - I thought it high time that I gave something back, and shared interesting discoveries that I've made along the way.

I also both returned to software engineering after a short hiatus, and have returned to studying computer science academically, so for me this is a chance to apply my learnings, revise my findings, and write that knowledge to my own cold storage.

Expecting the unexpected

In stark contrast to Monty Pythons Spanish Inquisition, I'll share what you can expect to find in this blog:

  1. Software engineering nuggets, I want to share interesting tid bits that crop up in my day to day life
  2. Computer science Concepts, I want to share interesting areas I'm studying (this is a great way for me to revise)
  3. General skills, I want to share skills around time management, calendar wrangling, inclusivity

With that...

Thanks again, and I'm confident this won't join the graveyard of wordpress, medium, and other blogs.
